description: Phase 5 Beta 1 'contract-completion remediation': close every real gap found by the 2026-07-02 post-Alpha-4 audit before entering feature freeze. No new feature surface — each story either completes a contract a shipped Alpha 4 PRD already promised (fsync-backed atomic writes and a single durable store under core/src/state per PRD-13; runProbe-backed HealthcheckRunner/UrlScanner/doctor/downloader/setup-readiness consumers per PRD-14 US-317; RedactionService-routed managed-file events, PathsService.managedFileLedger, and FileFormat reconciliation per PRD-18; @lando/renderer-lando actually owning the default renderer layer per PRD-12; the doctor NDJSON paths on the central StreamFrame seam per PRD-15 US-326; plugin setup.flags merged into lando setup, uninstall machine teardown + purge-safe resumable reports, and the bundled Podman machine path on macOS/Windows per PRD-01; an explicit release-automation decision per PRD-08) or closes spec §8/§12 drift no later phase owns (writable app/meta/global config surfaces, config translate detect/list/from/file, source-scoped includes update with --no-network, app shell host-mode default on ShellRunner with --no-history/--no-interactive, app logs --follow/--since, real meta global list/info/logs/restart, and real build-time version reporting). Stories follow the 5-PRD breakdown in spec/beta-1/prd-beta-1-{01..05}-*.md (US-372..US-395); each story has a corresponding US-XXX section with full TDD acceptance criteria. Source of truth: spec/{01..19}-*.md and the originating Alpha 4 PRDs cited per story; when a story deliberately re-scopes a shipped contract (FileFormat, release posture, shell default) the spec/PRD text and schema snapshot must move in the same change. Beta 1 additionally lands the agent-native feature wave (PRD-06..09, US-396..US-409), the last new feature surface before freeze: McpService + `lando mcp` serving allowlist-governed MCP tools generated from the LandoCommandSpec registry over stdio (spec 10.14, 8.2.6, 8.3 `mcpAllowed:`), host agent-context env forwarding into exec surfaces (spec 6.9.1, 7.4, 7.5), `lando open` / `app:open` (spec 8.2.5), the top-level `lando:` Landofile version constraint (spec 7.4), and the disposable tool runner `apps:scratch:run` / `lando run` with the bundled `toolbox` recipe (spec 21.10.3, 8.8.10). The four features were worked into the normative spec parts first; when a feature PRD and a spec part disagree, the spec part wins. Beta 1 additionally closes the runtime-bundle distribution gap (PRD-10, US-410..US-412): runtime bundles are published as immutable runtime-v<version> release assets on this repository (no separate runtime-bundles repo), the committed runtime-bundle-versions.json is pinned to real published artifacts so local builds and source runs complete `lando setup` with zero env overrides, and CI gates enforce the spec 5.8.1 committed-manifest invariant (no placeholder checksums, on-repo URLs only, version drift blocked).

userStories.title: Durable atomic writes fsync before rename
userStories.description: As a user whose machine loses power mid-write, no Lando-owned durable file (state store, cache, cwd-app map) is ever left partially written, because every atomic write path flushes to disk before the rename.
acceptanceCriteria: `writeFileAtomicScoped` (`core/src/state-store/atomic.ts`) fsyncs the temp file before rename; the rename only happens after a successful flush.
`writeFileAtomicViaRename` (`core/src/cache/atomic.ts`) gains the same fsync-before-rename behavior.
`core/src/cache/cwd-app-map.ts` no longer writes with raw `writeFile`; it routes through the atomic helper.
A unit test proves the temp file is flushed before rename (injected fs seam or flush-failure rejection).
`check:state-store-boundary` still passes; no new hand-rolled temp+rename paths are introduced.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-13 FR-3 ('temp + fsync + rename') — audit found zero fsync calls in either helper; cwd-app-map bypasses the helper entirely.

userStories.title: One durable-store implementation — retire json-bucket.ts
userStories.description: As a maintainer, all durable, versioned, lockable Lando-owned writes flow through `StateStore`, so envelope/lock/quarantine semantics exist in exactly one place.
acceptanceCriteria: The managed-file ledger (`core/src/managed-file/service.ts`) persists through `StateStore` (or a StateStore-backed bucket adapter) instead of `openJsonBucket`.

`core/src/state-store/json-bucket.ts`'s independent versioned-envelope + advisory-lock + quarantine implementation is removed or reduced to a thin delegating shim over `core/src/state/**` with no duplicated envelope/lock logic.
Existing ledger data written by the old bucket format is still readable (migration or format-compatibility test), or the ledger format is explicitly versioned forward with a documented migration.
The `check:state-store-boundary` allowlist entry for `core/src/state-store/json-bucket.ts` is removed or narrowed to the shim, and the `check:probe-boundary` advisory-lock allowlist is re-audited.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-13 FR-1 / success metric (exactly one durable-store implementation under core/src/state/). Coordinate with US-379/US-380 which touch the same service file.

userStories.title: Ship the runProbe-backed default HealthcheckRunner
userStories.description: As a service author, healthchecks declared in a Landofile run through a real `HealthcheckRunner` built on `runProbe`, with retry/delay/timeout mapped onto a `RetryPolicy` and verdicts mapped onto `ProbeOutcome`.
acceptanceCriteria: A live `HealthcheckRunner` implementation replaces `HealthcheckRunnerUnavailableLive` as the default wiring; the stub remains only as an explicit test/degraded-mode layer if still needed.
The implementation builds on `@lando/sdk/probe`'s `runProbe` — no hand-rolled `Effect.retry`/`Schedule` loop — and passes `check:probe-boundary`.
Healthcheck `retry`/`delay`/`timeout` config map onto `RetryPolicy` per §10.5; the runner's verdict maps onto `ProbeOutcome` (green/yellow/red).
`ProbeResult.lastError` is redacted through `RedactionService` before it reaches any event, transcript, or readiness summary.
Behavior is asserted deterministically under `TestClock` (attempt counts, backoff, timeout) with no wall-clock flake.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-14 US-317 — audit found `core/src/subsystems/healthcheck/api.ts` is an Unavailable stub and zero runProbe call sites exist in core/src.

userStories.title: Ship the runProbe-backed default UrlScanner
userStories.description: As a user running `lando start`, URL scanning ('is this route responding yet?') runs through a real `UrlScanner` built on `runProbe`.
acceptanceCriteria: A live `UrlScanner` implementation replaces `UrlScannerUnavailableLive` as the default wiring.
The implementation builds on `runProbe`, maps scanner config onto `RetryPolicy`, and returns `ProbeOutcome`-shaped verdicts per §10.5.
Outbound requests go through the `HttpClient` egress chokepoint (no ad-hoc fetch), honoring `network.proxy` / `network.ca`.
`ProbeResult.lastError` is redacted through `RedactionService` before leaving the scanner.
Deterministic `TestClock` coverage for attempts/backoff/timeout.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-14 US-317 — `core/src/subsystems/scanner/api.ts` is an Unavailable stub.

userStories.title: Migrate doctor, downloader, and setup readiness onto runProbe
userStories.description: As a maintainer, the remaining PRD-ALPHA4-14 US-317 consumers — DoctorService shell checks, the Downloader retry path, and `lando setup` readiness waits — run their retry/timeout-to-verdict loops through `runProbe`, and `lando doctor` wires the real subsystems.
acceptanceCriteria: `DoctorService` shell checks with retry/timeout semantics run through `runProbe`.

The `Downloader` retry path maps its retry policy onto `RetryPolicy` and executes attempts through `runProbe` (or documents, in the probe-boundary allowlist with justification, any loop that genuinely cannot map).
`lando setup` readiness waits (socket probe, provider readiness) consume `runProbe`.
`core/src/cli/commands/doctor-subsystems.ts` wires the real HealthcheckRunner/UrlScanner layers; the Unavailable stubs no longer appear in any default bootstrap path.
`check:probe-boundary` allowlist shrinks to only the documented state-lock loops; a hand-rolled retry loop reintroduced in any migrated consumer fails the gate.
Existing setup/doctor/downloader behavior tests stay green; migrated behavior is asserted under `TestClock` where timing-sensitive.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-14 US-317. Depends on US-374/US-375 for the real subsystem layers.

userStories.title: Runtime behavior test for waitForEvent
userStories.description: As a plugin author relying on `@lando/core/testing`, `waitForEvent` is proven at runtime to delegate to `EventService.waitFor` and honor the timeout contract, not just to exist as an export.
acceptanceCriteria: A runtime test in `core/test/testing/` proves `waitForEvent` resolves when a matching event is published.
A runtime test proves `waitForEvent` fails with the `EventError` `reason: "timeout"` contract when the deadline elapses, driven deterministically through `TestClock`.
The test asserts the returned event is the typed, redacted payload (parity with `EventService.waitFor` semantics).
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-14 FR-9 — audit found `waitForEvent` covered only by an export-existence assertion (`core/test/library/core-testing-export.test.ts`) while `expectEvent` has real runtime coverage.

userStories.title: Resolve the orphaned Mutagen downloader files
userStories.description: As a maintainer, the working tree carries no orphaned product files: the untracked `plugins/file-sync-mutagen/src/download.ts` and `plugins/file-sync-mutagen/test/download.test.ts` are either deleted (the shipped path is `provisionMutagen` in `plugins/file-sync-mutagen/src/provision.ts`) or deliberately wired and committed.
acceptanceCriteria: A decision is recorded in this story's notes: the bespoke downloader is redundant with the tool-provisioning path, or it serves a purpose provision.ts does not.
If redundant: both files are deleted; no production or test code references them.
If kept: `download.ts` is imported by the plugin's production path, its test runs in CI, and both files are committed.
`git status` is clean of these paths after the change (no untracked product files).
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: working-tree discipline (root AGENTS.md). These files already blocked one push (spec/alpha-4/progress.txt 2026-07-02 04:42 entry). Only referenced by each other; plugin entry imports provisionMutagen only.

userStories.title: Managed-file events route through RedactionService
userStories.description: As a security-conscious user, managed-file events and transcripts never contain unredacted secrets, because the service uses the canonical redaction registry instead of a local empty redactor.
acceptanceCriteria: `ManagedFileServiceLive` resolves `RedactionService` and uses it for every event payload and transcript line it emits; `createSecretRedactor([])` is removed from `core/src/managed-file/service.ts`.
A test proves a registered secret value appearing in a managed-file event payload is masked in the emitted event.
The `check:redaction-boundary` gate covers the managed-file event path (added to the gate's consumer inventory if it enumerates consumers).
`RedactionService` is provided directly to the sub-layer that needs it (per the Layer.mergeAll sibling-visibility gotcha in root AGENTS.md).
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-18 ('Every ManagedFile event and transcript MUST be routed through RedactionService'). Audit found a local empty redactor at core/src/managed-file/service.ts:911-943.

userStories.title: PathsService.managedFileLedger(appId)
userStories.description: As a plugin author or embedding host, I can resolve the managed-file ledger path for an app through `PathsService` / `@lando/core/paths` instead of re-deriving it.
acceptanceCriteria: `PathsService` (`sdk/src/services/paths.ts`) gains `managedFileLedger(appId: string)`; the pure `makeLandoPaths` peer gains the equivalent member.
`core/src/config/roots.ts`'s `managedFileLedger` helper delegates to (or is replaced by) the primitive; internal callers migrate to the sanctioned surface.
The addition follows sdk/AGENTS.md: `sdk/API_COMPATIBILITY.md` updated, `bun run codegen:schema-snapshot` refreshed, export/import-boundary tests updated.
`check:paths-boundary` passes; no hand-rolled ledger path joins remain.
Path-matrix tests cover the new member across platforms (win32/linux/darwin separators, overridden roots).
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-18 ('PathsService / @lando/core/paths gain a managedFileLedger(appId) derived path'). Audit found the helper only in core/src/config/roots.ts:19.

userStories.title: Reconcile FileFormat with the frozen contract
userStories.description: As an SDK consumer, the shipped `FileFormat` enum and the PRD-ALPHA4-18 contract say the same thing: either the JS/TS formats are formally adopted into the contract, or the schema is trimmed back to the frozen 7-value enum.
acceptanceCriteria: A decision is recorded in this story's notes: adopt `javascript`/`typescript` into the contract, or remove them from `sdk/src/schema/managed-file.ts`.

If adopted: `spec/alpha-4/prd-alpha-4-18-managed-files.md` (and §10.13 if it enumerates formats) is updated to the 9-value enum, codec behavior for both formats is tested (write/read round-trip, marker handling), and the schema snapshot reflects the adopted surface.
If trimmed: the two values are removed, no shipped consumer references them (verified by search + tests), and the schema snapshot is refreshed.
PRD text, schema, snapshot, and `sdk/API_COMPATIBILITY.md` agree after the change; no silent drift remains.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-18 freezes FileFormat as the 7-value enum; sdk/src/schema/managed-file.ts:18-28 ships 9 values. Check the introducing commit's intent before choosing trim vs adopt.

userStories.title: @lando/renderer-lando owns the default renderer layer
userStories.description: As a renderer-plugin author, I can read `@lando/renderer-lando` as the reference implementation: its exported renderer layer IS the default TTY renderer, and core wires it via the bundled-plugin mechanism rather than assembling it.
acceptanceCriteria: `plugins/renderer-lando`'s renderer export is the real default TTY renderer layer (task-tree painter, event consumer, plain/json fallbacks) — no more `Layer.empty` placeholder.

`core/src/cli/renderer/bundled-renderers.ts` no longer constructs the lando renderer from parts; it resolves the renderer contribution from the bundled plugin (shared primitives may live in `@lando/sdk/renderer`, but composition/ownership sits in the plugin).
Fallback modes (non-TTY plain, `--renderer=json`) and renderer selection order are preserved; existing renderer golden/visual-QA tests stay green (goldens may move, not weaken).
Cold-start discipline holds: the plugin's renderer layer stays off the cold-start static import graph and `@opentui/core` remains behind the dynamic-import boundary; import-boundary gates pass.
Bundled-plugin codegen is regenerated in the same change if the contribution shape changes; `git diff --exit-code` clean on generated paths after codegen.
`check:renderer-boundary` passes; dual-dispatch parity tests pass.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-12 renderer-plugin ownership AC. Audit: plugins/renderer-lando/src/index.ts:14 exports `renderer = Layer.empty` while core assembles the real renderer in bundled-renderers.ts.

userStories.title: Doctor NDJSON goes through the central StreamFrame seam
userStories.description: As an agent consuming `lando doctor` NDJSON, every emitted line is produced by the central redaction-aware StreamFrame encoder, uniform with every other streaming command.
acceptanceCriteria: `core/src/cli/commands/doctor-ndjson.ts` serializes frames through the central StreamFrame encode seam, not raw `JSON.stringify`.
`core/src/cli/commands/doctor-report.ts`'s doctor.check event lines go through the same seam.
Emitted NDJSON output stays byte-compatible with the frozen shapes (snapshot/conformance tests), or any deliberate shape change updates the frozen snapshot with justification.
A redaction test proves a registered secret in a doctor check result is masked in the NDJSON output.
Any machine-output-gate allowlist entries for these two files are removed.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-15 US-326 (migrate one-off NDJSON/event-line renderers onto StreamFrame). Audit found raw JSON.stringify call sites in both files.

userStories.title: Merge plugin setup.flags into lando setup
userStories.description: As a provider-plugin author, flags I contribute through `setup.flags` appear in `lando setup --help`, parse correctly, and reach my setup subscriber — on both the OCLIF source path and the compiled binary path.
acceptanceCriteria: Bundled provider plugins' `setup.flags` contributions are merged into the `meta:setup` command metadata (help output, flag parsing) at the static OCLIF command surface; runtime-discovered plugin contributions are collision-checked at plugin load and dynamic external-flag parsing is deferred to a later two-phase parser story.
Parsed plugin flag values are delivered to the contributing plugin's setup handler.
Parsing tests cover a contributed flag (presence in metadata, value round-trip, unknown-flag rejection), fulfilling PRD-ALPHA4-01 US-200 AC-4.
Source-dispatch and `runCompiledCli` behave identically (parity test), respecting the compiled-manifest constraints in core/AGENTS.md.
Cold-start budget holds: flag merging must not force plugin loading before the setup command actually runs.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-01 US-200 AC-4. Audit: sdk/src/schema/plugin.ts:153-202 declares setup.flags; meta/setup.ts:563-589 hardcodes built-in flags with no merge path. Beta 1 scope: expose bundled provider setup flags through the static OCLIF/compiled metadata surface, validate runtime-discovered plugin flag collisions at plugin load, and defer dynamic external-flag parsing to a later two-phase parser story.

userStories.title: Uninstall tears down Lando-owned provider machines
userStories.description: As a user running `lando uninstall --purge`, Lando-owned provider machines (the managed Podman machine/socket) are stopped and removed automatically, not listed for manual cleanup.
acceptanceCriteria: The uninstall plan executes teardown for provider machines Lando created (stop the managed socket/service, remove the machine/VM state) instead of marking `managed-provider-machines` as manual.
Machines not owned by Lando are never touched; ownership is determined from recorded setup state, and ambiguous ownership degrades to manual with remediation text.
The uninstall report records each machine action (removed / skipped-not-owned / failed with remediation).
Idempotency: re-running uninstall after a partial machine teardown converges without error.
Scenario tests cover owned-machine removal and not-owned skip on the test provider; live provider integration remains env-gated.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-01 uninstall contract. Audit: core/src/cli/commands/uninstall.ts:193-197 marks managed-provider-machines as manual. Depends on machine-identity state recorded by US-387.

userStories.title: Uninstall report survives purge failures
userStories.description: As a user whose `lando uninstall --purge` fails partway, I always get a resumable report telling me what was removed and what remains, even when `userDataRoot` itself is already gone.
acceptanceCriteria: The report writer no longer silently skips when `userDataRoot` was purged: the report is written to a fallback location that survives the purge, and its path is printed in the failure output.
The report content still enumerates completed, failed, and remaining steps with remediation, and a re-run consumes/reconciles it.
A test simulates a step failure after `userDataRoot` removal and asserts a resumable report exists at the fallback path.
Report writing routes through the sanctioned atomic-write helper (post-US-372).
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-01 US-206 ('Partial failures leave a resumable uninstall report'). Audit: uninstall.ts:320-322 skips the report when userDataRoot was purged.

userStories.title: macOS/Windows managed runtime uses bundled Podman machine tooling
userStories.description: As a macOS or Windows user, `lando setup` stands up the Lando-managed runtime with the bundled Podman machine tooling and the Lando-owned socket — never by falling back to system Podman machine tooling or a test environment variable.
acceptanceCriteria: The mac/win ensureRuntime path in `plugins/provider-lando/src/setup.ts` resolves the machine runner from the installed runtime bundle (Paths-driven binaries), removing the `makeSystemPodmanMachineRunner(undefined, …)` production fallback.

`process.env.LANDO_TEST_PODMAN_SOCKET` is honored only in explicit test wiring, never as a production fallback in the setup path (mirror the Linux socket-resolution precedence: explicit option > injected paths > managed default).

Machine lifecycle (init/start/socket resolution/restart-on-stale) works from the bundled tooling; recorded setup state includes the machine identity and socket path needed for idempotent re-detection and for US-385 teardown.
When the runtime bundle lacks machine tooling for the host platform, setup fails with a tagged remediation error naming the bundle/platform — it does not silently use system tooling.
Unit/scenario coverage runs against fake runners for both platforms; live verification is recorded via the provider-integration CI jobs.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-01 US-363..US-367 (runtime-socket autosetup). Audit: setup.ts:550-567 falls back to system machine runner + LANDO_TEST_PODMAN_SOCKET when no socket is injected. If partial mac/win scope is accepted for Beta 1, cut the scope in PRD text explicitly, not silently.

userStories.title: Release-automation posture is decided and wired
userStories.description: As a maintainer preparing 4.0.0-beta.N, CI can produce the full signed release (13-stage scripts/release.ts) — or, if beta releases are deliberately manual, that decision is recorded where the RC acceptance criteria will collect it.
acceptanceCriteria: A decision record lands (in this story's notes and the PRD-ALPHA4-08 open-questions section): full pipeline in CI for Beta, or manual invocation until RC.

If CI: `scripts/build-release-workflow.ts` emits a workflow that invokes the `scripts/release.ts` orchestrator with credential-gated signing stages (skipping cleanly with warnings when credentials are absent), committed via codegen with drift check clean.

If manual-until-RC: the release runbook documents the exact invocation, required credentials, and verification steps, and the generated workflow's dev-prerelease-only scope is stated in the workflow header comment.
The per-platform binary jobs and the release workflow reference the same platform id vocabulary (windows-x64 CI/release domain vs win32-x64 runtime host key preserved).
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: PRD-ALPHA4-08. Decision: Beta 1 runs the full signed 13-stage release manually until RC because signing credential ownership is unassigned and no signing secrets are wired into CI. The generated release workflow remains dev-prerelease-only; maintainers invoke scripts/release.ts per docs/release-runbook.md. Full-pipeline CI reopens at RC once credential owners are assigned.

userStories.title: Config write surfaces (app config / meta config / meta global config)
userStories.description: As a user, I can set, unset, edit, and validate configuration at all three scopes — app Landofile, global config, global-app stack — exactly as §8.2 specifies, with writes that are atomic and serializer-canonical.
acceptanceCriteria: `app config set/unset/edit/validate` operate on the app Landofile: set/unset accept the spec'd path grammar, `edit` opens $EDITOR with post-edit validation, `validate` reports schema errors with remediation.
`meta config set/unset/edit/validate` operate on global config with the same semantics.
`meta global config` gains the spec'd write verbs for the global-app stack.
Writes round-trip through the canonical `@lando/sdk/landofile` serializer (comment/format preservation per §7.8.1) and land via the atomic write helper (US-372).
Every write emits its result through the universal machine-output contract (faithful resultSchema, --json envelope) and honors --dry-run where the spec requires it.
Rejecting writes (invalid path, schema violation) fail with tagged errors + remediation, exit non-zero, and leave the file untouched.
Dual-dispatch parity tests cover the new verbs; command result schemas are frozen in the snapshot.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: spec §8.2.1/§8.2.2/§8.4 — audit found all three config surfaces read-only (plus a telemetry toggle).

userStories.title: app config translate detect/list/from/file flow
userStories.description: As a migrating user, `lando config translate` can detect the foreign config in my working tree, list supported translators, and translate from an explicit format or file — not just the cwd happy path.
acceptanceCriteria: Autodetection: with no args, the command probes registered ConfigTranslators' detect surfaces against the cwd and picks the unambiguous match; ambiguity fails with a list + remediation.
`--list` enumerates registered translators (id, source format) through the machine-output contract.
`--from=<id>` forces a translator; `--file=<path>` translates an explicit file.
Output composes with the existing `--write` flag and the §7.8.1 serializer; non-write mode prints through the renderer/JSON seam.
The translate command paths are exercised against the config-translator contract suite fixtures.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: spec §8.2.1 translate flow. Audit: core/src/cli/commands/app-config-translate.ts only supports cwd + --write.

userStories.title: Source-scoped app includes update with --no-network
userStories.description: As a user with many includes, I can refresh a single include source and run include updates offline against the lockfile/cache, per §8.2.3.
acceptanceCriteria: `app includes update [source]` accepts an optional source identifier and refreshes only that include; unknown sources fail with the known-source list.

`--no-network` performs the update strictly from cache/lock state, failing with remediation when a required artifact is absent — it never touches the network (asserted via the network-boundary test seam).
Default behavior (all sources, network allowed) is unchanged; `--check` composes with both new options.
Result schema extends faithfully (per-source outcomes) and is snapshot-frozen.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: spec §8.2.3. Audit: core/src/cli/commands/app-includes-update.ts always refreshes all includes; wrapper exposes only --check.

userStories.title: app shell spec parity (host default, ShellRunner, history/interactive flags)
userStories.description: As a user, `lando shell` behaves as §8.2.4 specifies: host mode is the default, host execution runs through the Bun-Shell-backed ShellRunner service, and --no-history / --no-interactive are honored.
acceptanceCriteria: Default mode is host; `--service <name>` selects service mode explicitly (per §8.2.4 precedence — read the spec verbatim before flipping; amend the spec first if ambiguous). The default flip is routed through the deprecation governance surface.
Host execution routes through the `ShellRunner` service (`core/src/services/shell-runner.ts`), not `child_process.spawn` in the command body — command bodies stay side-effect-free per spec §1.2.
`--no-history` prevents host shell-history persistence for the session; `--no-interactive` runs non-TTY (suitable for agents/CI) with deterministic exit-code propagation.
Interactive TTY behavior (signal forwarding, exit codes, terminal restore) is covered by a tmux-driven integration test or an equivalent PTY harness.
Dual-dispatch parity holds; renderer boundary passes.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: spec §8.2.4. Audit: core/src/cli/commands/shell.ts defaults to service mode and spawns host shells via child_process; flags limited to service/host.

userStories.title: app logs --follow and --since
userStories.description: As a user debugging a service, I can stream logs live (--follow) and bound them by time (--since), per §8.2.5.
acceptanceCriteria: `--follow` streams provider logs until interrupt, through the renderer in TTY mode and as StreamFrame NDJSON under --format json; cancellation cleans up the provider log stream under Scope.
`--since` accepts the spec'd duration/timestamp grammar and filters accordingly; invalid grammar fails with remediation.
Both compose with existing --service / --tail.
Provider capability is validated before planning: providers that cannot stream fail up front with a tagged error, not mid-stream.
Deterministic tests via the test provider's log source; live provider stream behavior covered in the env-gated integration suite.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: spec §8.2.5. Audit: logs.ts honors only service/tail; the OCLIF wrapper marks follow/since as Beta-only — Beta is now.

userStories.title: Real implementations for meta global list/info/logs/restart
userStories.description: As a user of the global app (shipped in Alpha 3), the global-app management commands actually work instead of returning deferred-stub errors.
acceptanceCriteria: `meta global list` enumerates global-app services with status through the machine-output contract.
`meta global info` reports the global app's resolved config/urls/services (parity with `app info` shape where §8.4 says so).
`meta global logs` supports the same surface as `app logs` (including US-393 flags) scoped to the global app.
`meta global restart` restarts the global app deterministically (stop+start semantics per §8.4).
The deferred-stub helper is no longer referenced by these four commands; compiled-dispatch branches exist and parity tests cover them.
Result schemas are added to the frozen snapshot; --json envelopes verified by the conformance suite.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: spec §8.4 / §18 (global app shipped in Alpha 3). Audit: all four are makeDeferredStubCommand stubs. Share implementation with app-scoped commands via the global AppRef; do not fork logic.

userStories.title: Real version reporting
userStories.description: As a user (or installer, or self-update flow), `lando version` and `lando --version` report the actual build version, embedded at build time — never 0.0.0.
acceptanceCriteria: `CORE_VERSION` (`core/src/version.ts`) and the `version` command result derive from the package version at build time: compiled binaries embed it via the build's define/injection step, and source dispatch reads the workspace package version.
`lando version` reports core, Bun, and platform truthfully in both dispatch modes; `lando --version` stays within the cold-start budget (< 80ms cold) with no Effect/OCLIF import creep.
The release pipeline's version stamping (scripts/release.ts) and this embedding agree — one source of truth; a release-stage test asserts the built binary prints the stamped version.
A guard test fails if CORE_VERSION regresses to the 0.0.0 placeholder in a built artifact.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Origin: spec §8.1/§17. Audit: core/src/version.ts is `export const CORE_VERSION = "0.0.0"` and core/src/cli/commands/version.ts hardcodes core: "0.0.0" with a TODO. Compiled-mode constraint: no import.meta.url for package metadata (core/AGENTS.md).

userStories.title: mcpAllowed command flag + mcp-allowlist cache + MCP SDK contracts
userStories.description: As a command author (core or plugin), I mark a command `mcpAllowed: true` and it becomes a default MCP tool, with the MCP schemas and tagged errors published from @lando/sdk.
acceptanceCriteria: `LandoCommandSpec` gains `mcpAllowed?: boolean` (default false) per spec 8.3; the shipped default opt-ins are the read-only/laterally-scoped set plus non-destructive lifecycle (`app:info`, `app:logs`, `apps:list`, `app:config get|view`, `meta:version`, `meta:doctor`, `apps:scratch:list`, `app:start`, `app:stop`, `app:restart`, `app:exec`).
The generated `mcp-allowlist` cache (spec 12.1 style, like `host-proxy-allowlist`) is produced from every spec with `mcpAllowed: true` and refreshed on the same triggers as the command indexes.
A destructive built-in (`app:destroy`, `apps:poweroff`, `meta:uninstall`, plugin mutations) declaring `mcpAllowed: true` is rejected at registration with `McpAllowlistConflictError`.

MCP tagged errors (`McpToolNotAllowedError`, `McpToolInputError`, `McpTransportError`, `McpAllowlistConflictError`) and the catalog/serve-options schemas are published from @lando/sdk; `sdk/API_COMPATIBILITY.md` updated and `bun run codegen:schema-snapshot` refreshed in the same change.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-06 (spec 10.14, 8.3). Mirror the hostProxyAllowed mechanics; do not invent a second allowlist shape.

userStories.title: McpService core: catalog projection + retained-runtime dispatch
userStories.description: As an AI agent, every allowlisted Lando command is a typed MCP tool whose input schema derives from the command's flags/args and whose result is the command's redacted CommandResultEnvelope.
acceptanceCriteria: `McpService` (`serve`, `catalog`) ships per spec 10.14: tools are generated from the LandoCommandSpec registry; no hand-maintained tool list exists.

Tool dispatch goes through the `@lando/core/cli` command operations against one retained LandoRuntime for the session (host-proxy dispatcher pattern, spec 10.10.1); successive calls hit hot-path budgets.

Tool results are produced by `encodeCommandResult` (spec 8.11.2) and pass through `RedactionService`; streaming commands surface StreamFrames as MCP progress notifications terminated by the result envelope.
Dispatch runs `interaction: "non-interactive"`; prompting commands fail with their standard missing-answer tagged error inside an `ok: false` envelope.
In-flight calls are capped (default 4, `mcp.maxConcurrent`); MCP cancellation maps to `Effect.interrupt`; closing the transport interrupts the serve scope and finalizes in-flight calls.
`pre-mcp-call` / `post-mcp-call` events publish for every dispatch including rejected ones, with redacted payloads.
Tooling tasks project as tools when enabled (config `mcp.tooling` / `--tooling`), dispatched through `runTooling` (spec 16.7).
`McpService` registers in @lando/core/services and the spec 3.4 table row semantics hold (Layer.suspend at level `plugins`; nothing constructs it unless requested).
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-06 (spec 10.14). Core-owned, not plugin-replaceable in v4.0.

userStories.title: meta:mcp command (lando mcp) over stdio
userStories.description: As a user, I point my agent's MCP client config at `lando mcp` and the agent can discover and call Lando tools; `lando mcp --list` shows me exactly what is exposed.
acceptanceCriteria: `meta:mcp` (top-level alias `mcp`, bootstrap `plugins`) serves MCP over stdio per spec 8.2.6; the serve loop runs until the client closes the transport or the process is interrupted.
The effective tool set is mcpAllowed defaults + `mcp.allow` + `--allow`, minus `mcp.deny` + `--deny`; requests outside it fail with `McpToolNotAllowedError`.
`--list` prints the effective catalog (id, summary, allowance source) through the machine-output contract and exits; serve mode is the interactive carve-out per spec 8.11.4.
`meta:mcp` sets neither `hostProxyAllowed` nor `recipePostInitAllowed`; tests assert both allowlist caches exclude it.
Dual-dispatch parity: registered in the OCLIF command class and the compiled registry/`runCompiledCli` switch; parity tests cover `--list` and serve-refusal-without-stdin cases.
Global config `mcp:` block ({allow, deny, tooling}) validates against the published global-config schema (spec 7.5).
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-06 (spec 8.2.6). stdio only in v4.0; HTTP transport is deferred and must not be precluded.

userStories.title: MCP contract suite + doctor check
userStories.description: As a maintainer, the MCP surface is protected by a contract suite and lando doctor can tell a user whether their MCP surface is healthy.
acceptanceCriteria: The spec 10.14 contract suite ships: catalog matches the allowlist caches; tool input schemas round-trip against FlagSpec/ArgSpec; success and failure dispatches return schema-valid envelopes; deny wins over allow; destructive self-allow rejection; non-interactive prompt failure; cancellation mid-call; concurrency cap; a known secret never crosses the transport.
`lando doctor` gains an MCP check: allowlist cache fresh, catalog generates cleanly, canary tool round-trip against the test runtime succeeds.
The suite runs against the test runtime per-PR; no live agent client is required.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-06 (spec 10.14 contract-suite bullet list).

userStories.title: Agent-context env forwarding into exec surfaces
userStories.description: As an AI agent driving Lando, my identity markers (CLAUDECODE, CURSOR_AGENT, OPENCODE, ...) travel into `lando exec`/`ssh`/service shells and providerExec tooling automatically, so in-container tooling keeps agent context.
acceptanceCriteria: The spec 6.9.1 default allowlist (CLAUDECODE, CLAUDE_CODE, CURSOR_AGENT, OPENCODE, COPILOT_CLI, GEMINI_CLI, AGENT, CI) forwards into `app:exec`, `app:ssh`, `app:shell --service`, and providerExec tooling tasks, per invocation only.
Forwarding is presence-gated (unset names inject nothing) and loses to explicit env: exec request > task/service declaration > forwarding (spec 8.5.3 slot 7).
Forwarded values are never baked into planned service env, never written to the app plan cache, and never appear in LANDO_INFO.
The in-container `lando` shim env filter (spec 10.10.3) appends the agent-context allowlist so runLando re-entries preserve the markers.
Forwarded values pass through RedactionService in `pre-provider-exec` event payloads and transcripts; `check:redaction-boundary` stays green.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-06 (spec 6.9.1). Exact names only; wildcard handling is US-401.

userStories.title: agentEnv config, off-switches, and audit surface
userStories.description: As a security-conscious user, I can extend, restrict, disable, and audit exactly which host env names cross into containers.
acceptanceCriteria: Global config `agentEnv:` ({enabled, allow, deny}) validates per spec 7.5; wildcards in allow/deny are rejected at config validation with `AgentEnvPatternError`.
Landofile top-level `agentEnv: false` opts one app out (spec 7.4); `LANDO_AGENT_ENV=0` disables forwarding for a single invocation.
`lando info --deep` reports the resolved allowlist (built-ins + allow - deny) so users can audit what crosses the boundary.
`AgentEnvPatternError` and the agentEnv config schema are published from @lando/sdk with the schema snapshot refreshed; `sdk/API_COMPATIBILITY.md` updated as required.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-06 (spec 6.9.1, 7.4, 7.5).

userStories.title: app:open (lando open) opens resolved app URLs
userStories.description: As a user, `lando open` opens my app's primary URL in my real browser, and `--service`/`--route`/`--all`/`--print` give me precise control.
acceptanceCriteria: `app:open` (alias `open`, bootstrap `app`) resolves targets from the app plan/ServiceInfo per spec 8.2.5: default = first proxy route of the first service that declares one, preferring https; `--service`, `--route <host>`, `--all` scope/override; the command never invents URLs.
No resolvable target fails with tagged `OpenTargetUnresolvedError` listing what app:info knows plus proxy-config remediation.
Only http/https URLs are opened; the invariant is asserted structurally and on violation fails with HostProxyOpenUrlSchemeError semantics.
The URL opens through the platform opener (xdg-open/open/start) via a ShellRunner-backed host-opener helper; a headless host degrades to printing the URL with a note and exit 0.
`--print` skips opening and prints resolved URL(s); `--format json` returns the resolved target list through the machine-output contract with a frozen resultSchema.
Resolution is hot-path friendly: cached plan/service info, no provider contact unless `--service` needs live endpoint state.
`pre-open-url` / `post-open-url` events publish per opened URL with redacted summaries; `cli-app:open-*` lifecycle events publish per spec 3.5.
Dual-dispatch parity tests cover the new canonical id; schema snapshot updated.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-07 (spec 8.2.5).

userStories.title: app:open host-proxy round-trip
userStories.description: As a developer inside a container shell, typing `lando open` still opens the URL in my host browser via the host proxy.
acceptanceCriteria: `app:open` registers `hostProxyAllowed: true`; the generated host-proxy allowlist cache includes it.
An in-container `lando open` forwards over the spec 10.10 runLando channel; the host dispatcher resolves against the retained runtime and performs the same target resolution and opener behavior.
The host-proxy contract suite gains coverage for the app:open round-trip (allowlist presence, dispatch, exit-code propagation).
Guide coverage exists for `lando open` (executable guide or existing guide updated with drift gate re-run).
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-07 (spec 8.2.5, 10.10).

userStories.title: Top-level lando: version constraint key
userStories.description: As a team lead, I commit `lando: ">=4.1 <5"` in our Landofile and teammates on an unsatisfying Lando version fail fast with clear remediation instead of failing weird.
acceptanceCriteria: The top-level `lando: <semver-range>` key validates per spec 7.4: unparseable ranges fail Landofile validation with `LandofileParseError` and range-syntax remediation.
Constraints are evaluated after the six-file merge and ACCUMULATE across layers: the running version must satisfy every declared range; a lower-precedence layer cannot loosen a stricter one.
Range evaluation includes prereleases (`>=4.1` is satisfied by `4.1.0-beta.2`).

An unsatisfied constraint fails closed with tagged `LandofileVersionConstraintError` carrying the declared range(s), each range's source layer, the running version, and remediation (`lando update` or edit the constraint).
`LANDO_SKIP_VERSION_CONSTRAINT=1` downgrades the failure to a renderer warning for the invocation.
The key, error, and semantics land in the published Landofile schema; snapshot refreshed and `sdk/API_COMPATIBILITY.md` updated as required.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-08 (spec 7.4). Distinct from runtime:/api:; mirrors recipe requires.lando (spec 8.8.3).

userStories.title: Version-constraint hot-path enforcement + doctor reporting
userStories.description: As a user running cached tooling commands, the version constraint is still enforced without provider contact, and lando doctor tells me when an app's constraint is unsatisfied or being skipped.
acceptanceCriteria: The resolved constraint set is carried in the app plan cache and re-checked on the `tooling` bootstrap hot path against the embedded `CORE_VERSION` without provider contact.
The hot-path perf budget stays green (benchmark gate unaffected by the check).
`lando doctor` reports any app whose constraint is unsatisfied or being skipped via LANDO_SKIP_VERSION_CONSTRAINT.
Guide coverage exists for the version constraint (team-workflow guide section with drift gate green).
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-08 (spec 7.4, 12.1).

userStories.title: Bundled toolbox canonical recipe
userStories.description: As a user with no Landofile, the bundled `toolbox` recipe gives `lando run` a default disposable tool container that never blocks on prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: A `toolbox` recipe ships under `recipes/toolbox/` per spec 8.8.10: one `type: lando` service on a version-pinned general-purpose CLI image; every prompt has a non-interactive default.
The recipe is wired through `scripts/build-bundled-recipes.ts` codegen into the binary; `bun run codegen` leaves `git diff --exit-code` clean on generated paths.
`meta:recipes:list` includes it; `meta:recipes:describe toolbox` and `meta:recipes:validate` pass.
Rendering it through the scratch pipeline (spec 21.4.2) with zero answers succeeds in --no-interactive mode.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-09 (spec 8.8.10, 21.10.3).

userStories.title: apps:scratch:run command core (lando run)
userStories.description: As a developer, `lando run composer install` spins a cwd-mounted toolbox container, runs the command, gives me its exit code, and cleans up completely - no Landofile required.
acceptanceCriteria: `apps:scratch:run` (aliases `scratch:run` and reserved bare `run`, bootstrap `scratch`) acquires via `ScratchAppService.acquire` with `source: from-recipe` (default `toolbox`, `--from` overrides), `isolate: "cwd"` + `mountCwd: <cwd>` (or `isolate: "baked"` with `--no-mount`), `detached: false`, recipe postInit skipped, per spec 21.10.3.

Everything after `--` passes verbatim to `RuntimeProvider.exec` in the target service (recipe primary service; `--service` selects; unknown service fails with `ScratchRunTargetError` listing available services) with TTY allocation when stdin is a TTY.
The container process exit code propagates as the command's exit code; a non-zero tool exit is NOT a tagged error.
Scope close destroys the scratch on normal exit, non-zero exit, and Ctrl+C (`Effect.interrupt`); `--keep` converts to detached after exec, prints the scratch id, and hands ownership to the registry.
The bare `run` top-level alias is reserved per spec 21.10.2: plugin/tooling claims are rejected with `CommandAliasConflictError`; user `commandAliases.custom` remap still works.
Dual-dispatch parity tests cover the new canonical id; `--answer` flows to recipe prompts as in apps:init.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-09 (spec 21.10.3). Thin layer over ScratchAppService - no new lifecycle machinery.

userStories.title: apps:scratch:run streaming output + agent env forwarding
userStories.description: As a script or agent, `lando run --format json` gives me well-formed NDJSON frames and a terminal result carrying the exit code and scratch id; my agent-context env rides into the exec.
acceptanceCriteria: The command declares `streaming` per spec 8.11.3; under `--format json` it emits StreamFrames and a terminal `result` frame carrying the exec exit code and the scratch id, with a frozen resultSchema in the snapshot.
stdout/stderr stream live through the renderer in TTY mode.
The agent-context env allowlist (spec 6.9.1) forwards into the exec; forwarded values are redacted in event payloads.
The machine-output conformance layer drives the command with `--format json` for success and failure cases.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-09 (spec 21.10.3, 8.11.3). Depends on US-400 for the forwarding primitive.

userStories.title: apps:scratch:run hardening: registry/gc, warm repeats, guide
userStories.description: As a user, disposable runs never leak resources, repeat runs are fast, and the feature is documented as an executable guide.
acceptanceCriteria: A `--keep` scratch appears in `apps:scratch:list` and is reaped by `apps:scratch:gc --prune`; an interrupted (Ctrl+C) run leaves no registry entries, containers, volumes, or scratch roots behind.
A second `lando run` against the same toolbox skips recipe re-resolution and image rebuild via the content-addressed recipe cache and buildKey short-circuit (spec 6.13.5), asserted by test.
Executable-guide coverage exists for `lando run` (guide scenario green, guide gates pass).
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Feature wave PRD-09 (spec 21.10.3, 21.11, 21.15 warm-pool non-goal).

userStories.title: Runtime-bundle publishing workflow (runtime-v* release series)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, bumping plugins/provider-lando/runtime-bundle-version triggers a generated workflow that assembles per-platform runtime bundles from pinned upstream sources and publishes them as immutable assets on a runtime-v<version> GitHub Release in this repository.
acceptanceCriteria: A new generated workflow (emitted by a scripts/build-runtime-bundle-workflow.ts-style generator following the build-release-workflow.ts pattern; never hand-edited YAML) builds runtime bundles for every supported host platform, keyed by runtime host key: linux-x64, linux-arm64, darwin-x64, darwin-arm64, win32-x64 (the §13.5 release platform id windows-x64 corresponds to manifest host key win32-x64; both names stay in their existing domains per root AGENTS.md).

Bundle contents are assembled from pinned upstream artifact versions declared in a committed input (not whatever the runner's package manager installed): Podman + rootless helpers on Linux; the Podman remote client + machine/gvproxy helpers on macOS and Windows. Re-running against the same pins produces artifacts with identical SHA-256s.

The workflow uploads the platform tarballs/zips (per the existing RUNTIME_BUNDLE_TARGETS naming) as assets on a runtime-v<version> GitHub Release in this repository, and fails rather than re-uploads if the tag or any asset already exists (immutability).
scripts/build-runtime-bundle.ts release mode derives asset URLs from this repository's releases/download/runtime-v<version>/ path (the dead lando/runtime-bundles base URL is removed).

The workflow's final step regenerates runtime-bundle-versions.json against the published assets and surfaces the diff for landing (PR or same-change commit per §17.8), so a version bump and its manifest pin always travel together.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: PRD-10 (spec §5.8.1 committed-manifest invariant, §13.5 in-repo hosting, §17.8 runtime-bundle publishing). darwin/win32 bundle artifacts here are repackaged pinned upstream releases; the mac/win machine lifecycle remains US-387.

userStories.title: Pin the committed manifest - local builds just work
userStories.description: As a developer, I can compile the binary locally (or run from source) and complete lando setup's runtime-bundle resolution with no LANDO_RUNTIME_BUNDLE_MANIFEST, no override flags, and no special setup - because the committed manifest pins real published artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: plugins/provider-lando/runtime-bundle-versions.json contains real HTTPS URLs under this repository's releases/download/runtime-v<version>/ path, real SHA-256 checksums, and real sizes for all five host targets; the _comment placeholder disclaimer is gone.
The manifest's runtimeVersion equals the runtime-bundle-version file's content.

A CI job (Linux) compiles the binary from the current checkout without LANDO_RUNTIME_BUNDLE_MANIFEST and runs lando setup end-to-end: the bundle downloads from the published runtime-v* asset, checksum-verifies, and installs. This published-manifest smoke check does not replace the §13.5 current-commit override verification, which remains as-is.

Running from source (bun run path) resolves the identical manifest via the existing static JSON import - asserted by a test that the embedded/imported manifest passes the production schema (https-only, no placeholders).
No resolution-precedence change: env override > paired flags > committed manifest, byte-identical behavior to §5.8.1.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: PRD-10. Depends on US-410 having published the first runtime-v release.

userStories.title: Committed-manifest invariant gates
userStories.description: As a maintainer, CI blocks any change that would regress the manifest to placeholders, off-repo URLs, or a version that drifts from the runtime-bundle-version file - so the local-builds-just-work property, once established, cannot silently rot.
acceptanceCriteria: A check script (wired into CI like the existing check:* gates) validates the committed manifest: every entry is HTTPS under this repository's releases/download/runtime-v<version>/ path, no placeholder checksums (all-zero-style SHA-256) and no sizeBytes: 0, the platform key set is exactly the five runtime host keys (linux-x64, linux-arm64, darwin-x64, darwin-arm64, win32-x64 - not the §13.5 release id windows-x64), and runtimeVersion matches plugins/provider-lando/runtime-bundle-version.

The release pipeline (scripts/release.ts) gains a release-blocking stage gate that fails when the manifest violates the invariant, mirroring the placeholder-rejection pattern already used for update-manifest binaries (isPlaceholderBinary).

Per-PR CI runs only the offline invariant checks (no network); the live verification that each manifest URL resolves over HTTPS to a 200 with the recorded Content-Length runs on the release path and a periodic job, per the §17.2 catalog row's offline/live gate split.
Placeholder-manifest regression is covered by a test fixture proving the check fails on the pre-US-411 placeholder manifest.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: PRD-10. Mirrors the isPlaceholderBinary pattern from core/src/cli/commands/update.ts.
